meaning: A trace record contains a sent office message, a received field message, and a trailing information line.
The information line contains timestamp, trace record type, code line number/name, and station number/name.
Control messages send only the control words that need to change, as [word number] [value] pairs.
Trace data values are bit-reversed from the code system database tabling order before device names are matched.
Unused bits are not shown in the trace bit-state display.
The trace bit-state display shows all indication bit states for the station even when the field only sends changed indication words.
A recall returns all indications for the station.
Data or checksum bytes starting with F are split into two transmitted bytes, such as F8 -> F0 08 and F0 -> F0 00.
FF is undefined and not allowed.
